search_text: Learning Active Camera for Multi-Object Navigation 学习用于多目标导航的主动相机 Getting robots to navigate to multiple objects autonomously is essential yet difficult in robot applications. One of the key challenges is how to explore environments efficiently with camera sensors only. Existing navigation methods mainly focus on fixed cameras and few attempts have been made to navigate with active cameras. As a result, the agent may take a very long time to perceive the environment due to limited camera scope. In contrast, humans typically gain a larger field of view by looking around for a better perception of the environment. How to make robots perceive the environment as efficiently as humans is a fundamental problem in robotics. In this paper, we consider navigating to multiple objects more efficiently with active cameras. Specifically, we cast moving camera to a Markov Decision Process and reformulate the active camera problem as a reinforcement learning problem. However, we have to address two new challenges: 1) how to learn a good camera policy in complex environments and 2) how to coordinate it with the navigation policy. To address these, we carefully design a reward function to encourage the agent to explore more areas by moving camera actively. Moreover, we exploit human experience to infer a rule-based camera action to guide the learning process. Last, to better coordinate two kinds of policies, the camera policy takes navigation actions into account when making camera moving decisions. Experimental results show our camera policy consistently improves the performance of multi-object navigation over four baselines on two datasets. 在机器人应用中，让机器人自主导航到多个物体是必要的，但也很困难。关键挑战之一是如何仅使用相机传感器有效地探索环境。现有的导航方法主要集中在固定相机上，很少尝试使用主动相机进行导航。因此，由于相机范围有限，代理可能需要很长时间才能感知环境。相比之下，人类通常通过环顾四周以更好地感知环境来获得更大的视野。如何让机器人像人类一样高效地感知环境是机器人学的一个基本问题。在本文中，我们考虑使用主动相机更有效地导航到多个对象。具体来说，我们将移动相机投射到马尔可夫决策过程中，并将主动相机问题重新表述为强化学习问题。然而，我们必须解决两个新的挑战：1）如何在复杂环境中学习良好的相机策略；2）如何将其与导航策略相协调。为了解决这些问题，我们精心设计了一个奖励函数，以鼓励智能体通过主动移动相机来探索更多区域。此外，我们利用人类经验来推断基于规则的相机动作来指导学习过程。最后，为了更好地协调两种策略，相机策略在做出相机移动决策时考虑了导航操作。实验结果表明，我们的相机策略在两个数据集的四个基线上持续提高了多对象导航的性能。 本文提出了一种利用主动相机提升多目标导航效率的方法，通过强化学习框架协调相机控制与导航策略。 Peihao Chen,Dongyu Ji,Kunyang Lin,Weiwen Hu,Wenbing Huang,Thomas H. Li,Mingkui Tan,Chuang Gan cs.CV,cs.RO

authors_text: Dasom Ahn,Sangwon Kim,Hyunsu Hong,Byoung Chul Ko
title: STAR-Transformer: A Spatio-temporal Cross Attention Transformer for Human Action Recognition
title_zh: STAR-Tr​​ansformer：用于人类动作识别的时空交叉注意力变压器
abstract: In action recognition, although the combination of spatio-temporal videos and skeleton features can improve the recognition performance, a separate model and balancing feature representation for cross-modal data are required. To solve these problems, we propose Spatio-TemporAl cRoss (STAR)-transformer, which can effectively represent two cross-modal features as a recognizable vector. First, from the input video and skeleton sequence, video frames are output as global grid tokens and skeletons are output as joint map tokens, respectively. These tokens are then aggregated into multi-class tokens and input into STAR-transformer. The STAR-transformer encoder layer consists of a full self-attention (FAttn) module and a proposed zigzag spatio-temporal attention (ZAttn) module. Similarly, the continuous decoder consists of a FAttn module and a proposed binary spatio-temporal attention (BAttn) module. STAR-transformer learns an efficient multi-feature representation of the spatio-temporal features by properly arranging pairings of the FAttn, ZAttn, and BAttn modules. Experimental results on the Penn-Action, NTU RGB+D 60, and 120 datasets show that the proposed method achieves a promising improvement in performance in comparison to previous state-of-the-art methods.
summary: In action recognition, although the combination of spatio-temporal videos and skeleton features can improve the recognition performance, a separate model and balancing feature representation for cross-modal data are required. To solve these problems, we propose Spatio-TemporAl cRoss (STAR)-transformer, which can effectively represent two cross-modal features as a recognizable vector. First, from the input video and skeleton sequence, video frames are output as global grid tokens and skeletons are output as joint map tokens, respectively. These tokens are then aggregated into multi-class tokens and input into STAR-transformer. The STAR-transformer encoder layer consists of a full self-attention (FAttn) module and a proposed zigzag spatio-temporal attention (ZAttn) module. Similarly, the continuous decoder consists of a FAttn module and a proposed binary spatio-temporal attention (BAttn) module. STAR-transformer learns an efficient multi-feature representation of the spatio-temporal features by properly arranging pairings of the FAttn, ZAttn, and BAttn modules. Experimental results on the Penn-Action, NTU RGB+D 60, and 120 datasets show that the proposed method achieves a promising improvement in performance in comparison to previous state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 在动作识别中，虽然时空视频和骨架特征的结合可以提高识别性能，但需要单独的模型和平衡跨模态数据的特征表示。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了时空交叉（STAR）变换器，它可以有效地将两个跨模态特征表示为可识别的向量。首先，根据输入视频和骨架序列，分别将视频帧输出为全局网格标记，将骨架输出为关节图标记。然后这些代币被聚合成多类代币并输入到 STAR-transformer 中。 STAR-transformer 编码器层由一个完整的自注意力（FAttn）模块和一个建议的锯齿形时空注意力（ZAttn）模块组成。类似地，连续解码器由 FAttn 模块和提出的二进制时空注意力（BAttn）模块组成。 STAR-transformer 通过正确排列 FAttn、ZAttn 和 BAttn 模块的配对来学习时空特征的有效多特征表示。 Penn-Action、NTU RGB+D 60 和 120 数据集上的实验结果表明，与之前最先进的方法相比，所提出的方法在性能方面取得了有希望的改进。

AI.tldr: 提出STAR-Transformer模型，有效融合视频和骨架特征进行动作识别
AI.motivation: 现有方法需独立处理跨模态数据且难以平衡特征表示，需更高效融合方法
AI.method: 设计包含全自注意力和时空注意力模块的STAR-Transformer，聚合视频和骨架特征为多类标记进行处理
AI.result: 在Penn-Action、NTU RGB+D 60和120数据集上优于现有方法
AI.conclusion: STAR-Transformer通过有效融合时空特征，提升动作识别性能，为跨模态学习提供新思路
